opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I was trying to read an email and suddenly I couldn't understand the words, my brain felt like it short-circuited, and I am completely exhausted.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It happened tonight after a bit of computer work — I'm scared it's a stroke or early dementia.

symptom_narrative.onset: came on suddenly after about 30 minutes of computer work tonight
symptom_narrative.duration: ongoing this evening, easing slowly with rest
symptom_narrative.character: couldn't understand/produce words, felt like a short-circuit, with a heavy weight in the head
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: very frightening; I keep losing words mid-sentence
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: it's in my thinking and language, plus whole-head heaviness
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: triggered by mental effort, then a crash
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: resting, closing my eyes, quiet and dark
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I have post-viral brain fog and get these cognitive crashes, but the word-finding tonight felt more severe

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: Long COVID with brain fog and memory problems

family_history_as_reported.narrative: nothing major that I'm aware of

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: my spouse is here and we're both frightened

communication_style.typical_message_style: slow, pausing to find words; spouse assists

objective_data_offered.value: spouse reports his face is symmetric, both arms lift equally, and speech is slow/word-finding but not slurred or one-sided
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: informal spouse-performed FAST check, not a clinical neuro exam

objective_data_offered.value: he is alert and knows who and where he is, just slow
